q: The world's reliance on electronics is very great.
A television receiver is an electronic appliance.
Electronic circuits contain active electrical components only.
Passive electrical components require a power source to operate.
The vacuum tube is an active electrical component.
The transistor is an active electrical component.
Integrated circuits are active electrical components.
The resistor is a passive electrical component.
The capacitor is a passive electrical component.
The inductor is an active electrical component.
The transformer is a passive electrical component.
The LDR is an active electrical component.
The thermistor is a passive electrical component.
The most important material in the electronic industry is the semiconductor.
The process of adding a tiny amount of impurity to an intrinsic semiconductor is called doping.
The conductivity of a semiconductor can be increased by doping.
Pentavalent impurities are also called donor impurities.
Trivalent impurities are also called acceptor impurities.
The majority charge carriers in n-type semiconductors are electrons.
The majority charge carriers in p-type semiconductors are holes.
A p-n junction diode allows current to flow in both directions.
A diode is in forward bias when the p-type is connected to the positive terminal.
A diode is in reverse bias when the p-type is connected to the negative terminal.
A diode can be used as a rectifier.
The process of converting AC into DC is called rectification.
A half-wave rectifier uses at least two diodes.
A full-wave bridge rectifier uses four diodes.
LEDs convert electrical energy into light energy.
An LED must be connected in reverse bias to emit light.
The LDR's resistance increases as light intensity increases.
The resistance of a thermistor changes with temperature.
NTC thermistors' resistance decreases as temperature increases.
The transistor was invented in 1947.
An n-p-n transistor has three regions: emitter, base, and collector.
The emitter is heavily doped compared to the base.
The base of a transistor is very thick and heavily doped.
A transistor can act as an electronic switch.
A transistor can be used as an amplifier.
Logic gates are the basic building blocks of digital systems.
The NOT gate is also known as an inverter.
An AND gate output is high only if all inputs are high.
An OR gate output is low if any input is high.
A NAND gate is a combination of an AND gate and a NOT gate.
A NOR gate output is high only if all inputs are low.
LCDs emit light directly.
LCDs use a backlight to produce an image.
LCDs consume much less power than LEDs.
The Zener diode is designed to operate in the breakdown region.
A capacitor allows DC to pass through it easily.
The integrated circuit (IC) contains many electronic components on a single chip.
